mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not positive in > 55% of months with rising PAYEMS, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, the zero lower bound constraint forces investors out of safe cash instruments and into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as liquidity floods risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal funds rate at 0.07% as of December 2011
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of trading days when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or positive returns do not significantly exceed baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) and decrease in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a coordinated supply-demand recovery where rising output is absorbed by re-employed workers, amplifying earnings expectations and driving positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a bivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises discount rates and debt-service costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 2%, positive 60-day returns occur in > 50% of months when FEDFUNDS rises, or the Rank IC within tightening regimes is positive over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 700 thousand units indicates a stalled construction sector and weak residential investment, foreshadowing lower aggregate demand and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 685 thousand units as of November 2011
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC is positive over any 3-year window when HOUST < 700.

mechanism_narrative: A contemporaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirms a virtuous cycle where consumer demand is matched by manufacturing output, reducing inventory risk and lifting profitability expectations for positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or either proxy alone explains the return equally well (incremental R-squared < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8% signals substantial slack in the labor market, depressing wage growth and consumer spending power, which drags on corporate earnings and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 8%, or Rank IC is positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of stagnant demand defined by GDPC1 annualized growth below 2%, a month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) confirms a contracting consumption base, leading to negative 60-day S&P 500 returns as earnings estimates are cut.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% and RSAFS monthly decline
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth is below 2%, RSAFS declines fail to produce negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the conditional Rank IC is positive over any 3-year window.
